html:
Agora Inc. (9AG1.F / API): The Voice AI Transport Layer Trading Near Cash

Agora is still treated like a failed pandemic video SDK stock. That is the wrong frame. The current business is a cash-rich, founder-led real-time engagement network that has moved from Clubhouse-era human-to-human audio/video into conversational AI, voice agents, AI-native devices, live shopping, and multi-agent collaboration.

The lazy thesis used to be “negative EV, no debt.” That is stale. The official 2025 numbers show $374.9 million of cash, bank deposits, and bank financial products, but also $80.4 million of long-term borrowings tied to the Shanghai headquarters project and a $20.6 million project advance. Still, the economic point survives: at 9AG1.F around €3.14 and API around $3.89, the market is valuing the operating business at only a few tens of millions of dollars after cash-like assets. Cash-like assets were $4.29 per ADS at year-end, above the U.S. ADR price. Book equity was $562.8 million, versus an equity value in the low $300 million range. This is not a normal software multiple. It is an asset-backed option on a business inflection.

The inflection is now visible. In Q4 2025, revenue grew 10.7% year over year to $38.2 million, FY2025 revenue grew 5.9% to $141.1 million, net income flipped to $9.5 million from a $42.7 million loss, and operating cash flow turned positive at $27.2 million. The global Agora division grew 16.1% for the year, active customers rose 21%, and dollar-based net retention reached 109%. That is the key tell. Shengwang China is still softer, but even there retention improved to 89%.

What the market is missing is that Agora’s asset is not “video calling.” It is the real-time session layer: low-latency voice, routing, jitter management, interruption handling, global delivery, device SDKs, compliance, and scale. Voice AI agents need that layer. Model providers can generate voices, but enterprise deployments still need real-time orchestration across mobile apps, web, PSTN, devices, contact centers, and degraded networks. Agora’s Conversational AI push is aimed directly at customer service, sales, marketing, debt collection, surveys, and real-time support.

Recent customer and ecosystem signals are stronger than the headline revenue growth. Agora and FPT launched a Southeast Asia banking AI partnership where FPT already serves more than 40 banks and financial institutions. Agora and thymia are embedding real-time health and safety voice intelligence into live calls, with Agora citing 80 billion monthly engagement minutes, 200+ global points of presence, 99.999% uptime, and SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA certifications. The Convo AI Device Kit extends the same stack into robots, toys, smart hardware, and IoT devices. Agnes AI selected Agora Chat for AI group chat and multi-agent collaboration. Whatnot used Agora for a MrBeast event with roughly 600,000 peak concurrent viewers. These are not all material revenue events yet, but they show where usage can come from.

The stock has almost no retail heat. StockTwits shows only about 2,700 watchers for API, and Reddit mentions are mostly automated filing/news posts or developer discussions about latency, barge-in, PSTN, and production voice-agent pain points. That matters. This is not an over-owned AI name. It is a tiny, confusing ADR that screens as a Chinese microcap with mediocre trailing growth, while the business is quietly becoming a model-neutral voice AI infrastructure layer.

The biggest accounting nuance is that headline free cash flow is muddied by headquarters construction. Operating cash flow is the better near-term gauge. FY2025 OCF was $27.2 million, while the company spent $31.9 million on construction in progress and repurchased $27.7 million of stock. The buyback is material: Agora has repurchased $143.1 million of ADS-equivalent stock since 2022 and extended the program through February 2027. Retiring stock below cash per ADS is highly accretive if the business is no longer burning cash.

The bear case is straightforward: OpenAI, LiveKit, Twilio, cloud vendors, or in-house stacks commoditize the transport layer; AI costs pressure gross margin; management spends too much on hardware and China projects; Shengwang stays weak; and Q1 gives no hard AI revenue disclosure. China regulatory and cash-transfer risk also remains, though the old VIE structure was terminated in January 2025, reducing one major complexity.

But the setup is asymmetric because the base case does not require heroic growth. If 2026 revenue grows low double digits to roughly $158 million, operating loss narrows to breakeven, interest income keeps GAAP earnings positive, and AI/customer-service/device use cases begin contributing visibly, the market can value Agora at 2.5x forward revenue plus discounted net cash. That supports a stock around €6.75. If AI voice agents become a real production category and Agora’s global division sustains 20%+ growth with rising retention, $220-250 million of revenue within several years is plausible. At 4x sales plus net cash, the stock can trade into the low teens.

The next hard catalyst is Q1 2026 results after the U.S. close on May 26, 2026. The market will look for whether the $36-37 million revenue guide was conservative, whether Agora global retention stays above 100%, whether management breaks out conversational AI traction, and whether cash per ADS remains stable after buybacks.

Current 9AG1.F price: €3.14. Current API ADR price: $3.89. Frankfurt is thin, so API is the cleaner price-discovery line.

Bear target: €2.05, downside of 35%.
Base target: €6.75, upside of 115%.
Bull target: €12.50, upside of 298%.
